topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of the hammer hitting the wooden post was a sharp, jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. I stood frozen by the window, my breath catching in my throat. Every nerve ending seemed to have tightened into a single, taut wire. Across the street, Mr. Henderson was systematically erecting a new boundary, and the sheer, relentless activity felt invasive. I kept my eyes fixed on the manicured lawn, willing myself to become invisible. My hands were clammy, and I found myself gripping the edge of the windowsill so tightly my knuckles were white. *He’s too loud,* a small, panicked voice whispered in my mind. I started pacing the small living room, the floorboards seeming to creak under my sudden, unsteady movements, desperately searching for an escape route that didn't exist.

[story 2]

"Just a little longer, folks!" Mr. Henderson boomed, his voice carrying easily over the suburban stillness. I flinched, pulling my cardigan tighter around myself as if a physical barrier could ward off the noise. My gaze darted from the construction site to the thin gap in my own property line, a space that suddenly felt dangerously exposed. A cold slickness crawled up my spine. I retreated to the kitchen, the cool tile offering little solace. I stared at the reflection in the dark glass of the oven door—a pale, wide-eyed stranger. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat that threatened to spill out. I began counting the tiles on the counter, a pointless, obsessive task meant to anchor me against the rising tide of unease washing over me.

[story 3]

The rhythmic thudding of the carpentry tools was relentless, a steady, heavy pulse that resonated deep in my chest. I huddled beneath the heavy velvet curtains, trying to shrink my silhouette against the light. Each swing of the hammer seemed amplified, each splintering sound a sharp jolt to my system. I could feel a strange pressure building behind my eyes, making the world swim slightly. When Mr. Henderson paused to wipe his brow, his shadow fell momentarily across my doorway, and for a second, I couldn't move. My mouth went dry. I swallowed hard, the action thick and difficult. I focused intensely on the intricate pattern of the wallpaper, willing the details to hold my attention, anything to keep the internal tremor from escalating into something overwhelming.
